key_insight: Modified SINNs inject spectral structure into neural networks, improving high-dimensional PDE approximation by learning in the spectral domain with coefficient-decay scaling and basis embeddings.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a scalable alternative to classical spectral solvers and PINNs for high-dimensional PDEs, with better accuracy and lower memory use when dimensionality is large.
method: Operate directly in spectral space to avoid explicit spatial derivative computation.
Add coefficient decay scaling to better capture unknown spectral coefficients.
Use harmonic-analysis-inspired basis embeddings to strengthen approximation in high dimensions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on steady and time-dependent PDEs showing superior accuracy over sparse-grid spectral methods in middle dimensions and over PINNs in high dimensions.
watch_item: Performance depends on spectral information quality and implementation details; incomplete coefficients or poor basis/scaling choices may reduce gains.
paper_title: Spectral-Informed Neural Networks Outperform Spectral Methods in High-dimensional PDEs
